portwatch_chokepoints.note: Daily AIS-derived tanker counts. Anomaly = 7-day mean below threshold vs pre-crisis baseline OR 30d avg (ports avg>=5). T6-S45: 7d mean (was single-day, smooths ~5d feed lag) + strategic ports alert at -40% (0.60), others -50% (0.50).

items.title: EU Sets Target To Double Electrification Rate, Cut Oil & Gas Use
items.summary: The European Union has unveiled the Electrification Action Plan, aiming for electricity to account for 46% of total energy consumption within the bloc by 2040, effectively doubling the current rate. Spearheaded by the European Commission, the aggr...

items.title: India Is Becoming the World's Refining Swing Producer
items.summary: For years, the world's swing oil producer has been Saudi Arabia. Need more crude? Riyadh opens the taps. Too much oil? Riyadh closes them. But while everyone has been watching who controls crude production, another swing producer has quietly been...

items.title: Iraq Shuts Down Khor Mor Gas Field Amid Security Threats
items.summary: United Arab Emirates’ Dana Gas has suspended operations at all main production facilities in the Khor Mor gas field due to credible security threats amid an increasingly volatile regional situation. The decision to close the facility was made in c...

items.title: Iran Instructs Houthis to Prepare Red Sea Shipping Attacks
items.summary: Iran has instructed Yemen's Houthi movement to stand ready to close the Bab el-Mandeb strait, the vital gateway to the Red Sea, if the United States follows through on threats to strike Iranian power infrastructure. According to senior Iranian ‌so...

items.title: Could Somalia See The World's Next Major Oil Discovery?
items.summary: As the Gulf (the world’s traditional energy centre) becomes riskier, the oil industry is searching for new hydrocarbon provinces. Much high-impact exploration is concentrated in Latin America and West Africa, far from Asia-Pacific, the main oil-de...

items.title: The Undisputed Global Oil Superpower
items.summary: The newly released 2026 Statistical Review of World Energy reinforces a point that sometimes gets lost in political discussions about energy: the United States remains the world’s largest oil producer. But the size of the U.S. lead depends on what...

items.title: Asian LNG Spot Prices Jump 10% as Hormuz Crisis Reignites Supply Fears
items.summary: Spot prices of liquefied natural gas in Asia have surged by 10% in the past week to the highest level since March, as the re-escalation in the Middle East and the fresh near-halt to shipping through the Strait of Hormuz rekindled fears of supply i...

items.title: Pakistan Pays Highest Spot LNG Price in Four Years as Qatar Supply Falters
items.summary: State-controlled Pakistan LNG Ltd has bought the most expensive cargo of liquefied natural gas on the spot market in four years as the recent re-escalation of the Hormuz crisis cut off cargoes from its term supplier, Qatar. The state-owned Pakista...

items.title: TotalEnergies Sees Stronger Q2 Profit as Refining and Oil Trading Surge
items.summary: TotalEnergies expects higher cash flows from its oil production and strong refining margins and oil trading results to boost second-quarter profits compared to the prior quarter, as oil prices rose and fuel markets tightened in the wake of the Ira...

items.title: India Urges Shipowners to Avoid Deploying Nationals on Hormuz Voyages
items.summary: India has advised ship owners and managers to avoid hiring Indian nationals on vessels they plan to send through the Strait of Hormuz, as attacks on commercial vessels have intensified in the area since the U.S.-Iran ceasefire all but collapsed. I...

items.title: India Hikes Diesel and Jet Fuel Export Tax
items.summary: India has hiked the export duties it will be charging on exports of diesel and jet fuel for the two weeks starting July 16, as the return of the Hormuz crisis is set to further tighten fuel markets. The export taxes on diesel and aviation fuel wer...

items.title: IEA Warns World Has Just Weeks to Avoid Hormuz Economic Shock
items.summary: The world has just a few weeks before taking an economic toll from the re-escalation in the Middle East and the new halt to tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, according to Fatih Birol, the Executive Director of the International Energy A...

thermometer.ethical_notes: Public posts only · No PII storage · ≥3 sources required for alert · 24h cooldown · alerts NEVER replace official sources · TOS-compliant

resilience.disclaimer: Indice experimental heuristico. Ref: INFORM, FEMA NRI, ND-GAIN.

comunicacoes.label: Comunicacoes
comunicacoes.note: Tema severamente sub-instrumentado: 1 de 4+ indicadores presentes.

defesa.label: Defesa
defesa.note: Tema severamente sub-instrumentado: incendios e o gap mais critico (PT/Norte alto risco).

themes.note: Decomposicao em 8 temas da enciclopedia (T6-S26). Score=None quando dados insuficientes. Anti-synthetic: nao fabrica scores. Cada tema lista gaps_to_close_priority com APIs livres a integrar em S27+.
